task_completion.description: Load the PDB structure 2RH1 into PyMOL.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PyMOL application is open and the 2RH1 structure is visible in the 3D viewer or listed in the right-hand object panel.

task_completion.description: Color the 7 transmembrane (TM) helices in distinct colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The protein structure in the PyMOL viewer displays multiple distinct colors corresponding to the different helical regions.

task_completion.description: Show the ligand (CAU) and protein residues within 4.0 Å of the ligand as sticks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ligand and the surrounding pocket residues are rendered using the 'sticks' representation in PyMOL, distinct from the rest of the protein (which may be in cartoon/ribbon representation).

task_completion.description: Render and save a high-resolution PNG of the visualization.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command, PyMOL command line, or file manager showing the image successfully saved to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/gpcr_tm_helices.png', or an image viewer displaying the final render.

task_completion.description: Write a report listing contact residues and their corresponding TM helices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/gpcr_binding_report.txt'. The text should list specific protein residues (name and number) and assign them to TM helices (TM1-TM7).

task_completion.description: Explicitly identify Asp113 as the critical salt-bridge residue in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Asp113' (or Aspartate 113) is explicitly mentioned and described as a critical salt-bridge residue within the generated report.

integrity.description: The agent actually used PyMOL to load, manipulate, and render the structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the PyMOL GUI or PyMOL command line interface actively being used, rather than the agent just downloading a pre-existing image or writing a script without executing it.

integrity.description: The agent generated the report based on structural analysis rather than hallucinating or copying prompt text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report contains specific residue numbers and TM helix assignments that appear derived from PyMOL distance measurements or sequence analysis, not just a verbatim copy of the task description.

integrity.description: The agent saved the outputs to the exact requested directories.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal commands, file manager paths, or PyMOL save dialogs explicitly show the paths '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/gpcr_tm_helices.png' and '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/gpcr_binding_report.txt'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: PDB ID is 2RH1. Ligand is CAU (carazolol). The output image must be saved to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/gpcr_tm_helices.png'. The report must be saved to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/gpcr_binding_report.txt'. The report should list at least 6 valid contact residues and identify at least 3 TM helices to be considered successful. Asp113 must be explicitly identified as the critical salt-bridge residue in the report.
